en: But you don't actually have to keep doing that.
ko: 하지만 계속 그렇게 할 필요는 없어.

en: Oh, force of habit.
Here you go. Swiped these from the tech fridge.
Oh, nice going.
Want a slice?
Sure, thanks.
Cheers.
Cheers.
I noticed a couple hours ago you had a meeting with another possible CEO.
ko: 몇 시간 전에 다른 CEO 후보와 회의한 걸 봤어.

en: I saw him arrive. How'd that go?
ko: 그가 도착하는 걸 봤어. 어떻게 됐어?

en: It was going well.
Until he called us to leave.
The term he used was a chick site.
And I didn't hear anything he said after that.
ko: 그 이후로는 그의 말을 듣지 못했어.

en: Apparently, selling clothes makes us a chick site.
ko: 아무래도 옷을 파는 게 우리를 여성 사이트로 만든대.

en: They weren't really?
How is this all legit?
I couldn't agree more. I find that surprising.
Really? Sexism in business?
So, what'd you do for work, then, before you retired?
I was a VP for Dex One.
Phone books?
No. I was in charge of printing. Before that, I ran sales.
ko: 아니. 인쇄를 담당했어. 그 전에 영업을 했어.
